+        Parameters

+            List of species (e.g. Si II, Ca II, etc.) that the user wants to show as unique colours.
+            Species can be given as an ion (e.g. Si II), an element (e.g. Si), a range of ions

+        packets_mode : str, optional
+            Packet mode, either 'virtual' or 'real'. Default is 'virtual'.
+        nelements : int, optional
+            Number of elements to include in plot. The most interacting elements are included. If None, displays all elements.
+
+        Raises
+        ------

+            If species list contains invalid entries.
+

Describe the feature

More information about VSO data acquisition status.

### Proposed solution

The VSO has a "health report" page here: https://docs.virtualsolar.org/wiki/VSOHealthReport . It would be useful for the user to be able to have a link to this health report when Fido results that use `VSOClient` are shown. This would look something like what is the typical output shown in https://docs.sunpy.org/en/stable/tutorial/acquiring_data/index.html . I have added in an extra line:

```
import astropy.units as u

Fido.search(a.Time('2012/3/4', '2012/3/4'), a.Instrument.aia,
            a.Wavelength(171*u.angstrom))  
<sunpy.net.fido_factory.UnifiedResponse object at ...>
Results from 1 Provider:

Latest VSO data retrieval status available at https://docs.virtualsolar.org/wiki/VSOHealthReport
1 Results from the VSOClient:
```
This would at least inform the user as to the recent performance of the VSO with regard to their query.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sunpy/sunpy/issues/7853/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sunpy/sunpy/issues/7853/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sunpy/sunpy/issues/comments/2459506862","html_url":"https://github.com/sunpy/sunpy/issues/7853#issuecomment-2459506862","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sunpy/sunpy/issues/7853","id":2459506862,"node_id":"IC_kwDOACEKh86SmRiu","user":{"login":"rudrakshnalbalwar","id":126509395,"node_id":"U_kgDOB4phUw","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/126509395?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/rudrakshnalbalwar","html_url":"https://github.com/rudrakshnalbalwar","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/rudrakshnalbalwar/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/rudrakshnalbalwar/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/rudrakshnalbalwar/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/rudrakshnalbalwar/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/rudrakshnalbalwar/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/rudrakshnalbalwar/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/rudrakshnalbalwar/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/rudrakshnalbalwar/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/rudrakshnalbalwar/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-11-06T11:33:06Z","updated_at":"2024-11-06T11:33:06Z","author_association":"NONE","body":"seems interested can I work on this issue

Describe the feature

More information about VSO data acquisition status.

### Proposed solution

The VSO has a "health report" page here: https://docs.virtualsolar.org/wiki/VSOHealthReport . It would be useful for the user to be able to have a link to this health report when Fido results that use `VSOClient` are shown. This would look something like what is the typical output shown in https://docs.sunpy.org/en/stable/tutorial/acquiring_data/index.html . I have added in an extra line:

```
import astropy.units as u

Fido.search(a.Time('2012/3/4', '2012/3/4'), a.Instrument.aia,
            a.Wavelength(171*u.angstrom))  
<sunpy.net.fido_factory.UnifiedResponse object at ...>
Results from 1 Provider:

Latest VSO data retrieval status available at https://docs.virtualsolar.org/wiki/VSOHealthReport
1 Results from the VSOClient:
```

**Type:**  :rocket: `feature` | :sun_with_face:  GSOC

PR on First Objective of Tardis Benchmarking GSoC'24  

This code sets up a benchmarking environment for evaluating the performance of TARDIS, a simulation tool for astronomical phenomena. It measures the time taken for key operations like setting up the simulation, running TARDIS, and generating a convergence plot for data analysis. By timing these functions, it helps assess how efficiently TARDIS performs simulations and visualizes results, providing insights into potential optimization areas.

![Screenshot from 2024-04-01 17-54-39](https://github.com/tardis-sn/tardis/assets/126509395/520d436d-3a36-46a8-ab59-7d9c392af427)

Description: Addressing Inconsistent File Path Handling in tardis
Description:

This pull request fixes a bug in tardis related to how file paths are handled in tardis/io/atom_data/util.py and potentially other parts of the codebase.

Problem:

The variable fname in resolve_atom_data_fname can be either a string representing a filename or a path object. When fname is a path object, attempting to use the replace method on line 38 leads to an error because path objects have their own replace method. This inconsistency in data types causes unexpected behavior.

Solution:

This pull request implements the following fix:

Explicit Type Conversion (Recommended): The code is modified to ensure fname is always a string before applying the replace method. This is achieved by checking if fname is an instance of os.PathLike (a common base class for path-like objects) and converting it to a string using str(fname).
Benefits:

Fixes the bug causing errors when fname is a path object.
Improves code clarity and maintainability by ensuring consistent data types.
Testing:

Unit tests (if available) should be updated to verify the fix.
Manual testing can be performed to ensure the functionality of resolve_atom_data_fname and related functions is not affected.
Additional Notes:

Similar issues in other parts of the codebase (e.g., tardis/simulation/base.py) might need to be addressed.
Consider using type hints (PEP 484) to explicitly define expected data types for function arguments and return values.

Please review this pull request and let me know if you have any questions.
